description: Lock the 2026-06-24 rescope of double_hulled_ship_designs to Sir William Petty's documented 1662 double-bottomed or twin-hulled sailing experiments.

checks.reason: The node is now anchored to Petty's first twin-hulled prototype and Royal Society/National Maritime Museum evidence beginning in 1662, not to the 1569 Mercator projection placeholder.
Petty's documented double-bottomed hull experiment is not sourced as a galleon-design derivative.
Mercator projection is map/navigation context, not an evidenced direct prerequisite for the paired-hull vessel architecture.
Dry-compass navigation is adjacent seafaring context, not source-backed as a direct dependency for Petty's hull experiment.
The Royal Society record describes examination of the structure and sailing of Petty's new double-bottomed ship.
